description: Tier 3 batch test 3 results - ALL SERVICES JS_BLOCKED by APF Strategy D
critical_finding: APF 503 Strategy D (h2_end_stream=1) causes ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR on ALL JS resources for ALL registered AI services. This is a systematic regression blocking page loads, not targeted API interception.

results.details: Page HTML loaded but all JS chunks (webpack, framework, main, _app, index, _buildManifest) failed with ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. DevTools Network tab confirmed multiple .js requests returning net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. Page non-functional - no UI rendered.
Page loaded as completely blank white page. JS resources blocked by APF causing ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. No UI elements rendered.
Page loaded as completely blank white page. JS resources blocked by APF. Same ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR pattern as all other services.
Redirected to v0.app. Page loaded as completely blank white page. JS resources blocked by APF causing ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR.
Page HTML loaded (tab title shows Wenxin Yiyan), but app stuck on loading spinner indefinitely. JS resources blocked by APF causing ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. App cannot initialize.

summary: 5/5 services FAILED. All Tier 3 services are completely non-functional due to APF Strategy D blocking JS resources via ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. This confirms the systematic regression: APF is not selectively blocking API POST requests with sensitive data - it is blocking ALL HTTP/2 resources for registered service domains.
recommendation: URGENT: APF must be changed to only intercept API POST endpoints containing sensitive keywords, NOT all HTTP/2 requests to registered domains. Current Strategy D makes all registered AI services completely unusable.
